sections.title: Overview
sections.content: **Microsoft Entra ID** connector — `CONNECTOR_TYPE = "entra_id"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2"`. Exposes 20 public async methods. Connects to Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) via the Microsoft Graph API for identity and access management: users, groups, applications, service principals, directory roles, audit logs, and sign-in logs. Uses the OAuth2 client-credentials grant against a tenant-scoped token endpoint.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
1. Provision credentials in the Microsoft Entra ID dashboard.
2. In ACP → Manage Connectors → Microsoft Entra ID, fill in the install fields below.
3. Click Install — the connector validates required config keys via `install()`.
4. The gateway calls `health_check()` to confirm credentials are valid.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content: Auth type: **oauth2**. Credentials live in `self.config`, read via `self.config.get(...)`. Never hardcoded.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
Install fields (per-tenant):

| Key | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tenant_id` | string | yes | Your Microsoft Entra ID tenant ID (GUID) — from Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Overview |
| `client_id` | string | yes | From Azure Portal → App registrations → your app → Application (client) ID |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | From App registrations → Certificates & secrets → Client secrets |
| `scopes` | string | no | Microsoft Graph scope. Use the default (.default) for client-credentials apps so consented application permissions are honored. |
| `base_url` | string | no | Microsoft Graph API base URL. Override only when targeting national cloud endpoints (US Gov, China, Germany). |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | Maximum Microsoft Graph requests per minute. Graph throttles per-app and per-tenant; 240 is a safe default for most workloads. |

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content:
20 public async methods. See `metadata/connector.json::apis` for full param + return docs.

### `async install()`
Validates required config (tenant_id, client_id, client_secret) and marks the connector as installed.
### `async authenticate()`
Mints a Microsoft Graph access token via the OAuth2 client-credentials grant against the tenant-scoped token endpoint.
### `async health_check()`
Verifies Microsoft Graph connectivity by fetching the tenant's /organization resource.
### `async list_users(top, filter, search, orderby, select)`
Lists directory users with optional $filter, $search, $orderby, $select, and $top.
### `async get_user(user_id_or_upn, select)`
Fetches a single user by id or userPrincipalName.
### `async create_user(account_enabled, display_name, mail_nickname, password, user_principal_name, force_change_password_next_signin)`
Creates a new directory user with passwordProfile.
### `async update_user(user_id, fields)`
PATCHes arbitrary fields on a user.
### `async delete_user(user_id)`
Deletes a user.
### `async reset_user_password(user_id, new_password, force_change_next_signin)`
Administratively resets a user's password via PATCH on passwordProfile.
### `async list_groups(top, filter, select)`
Lists directory groups.
### `async get_group(group_id)`
Fetches a single group by id.
### `async create_group(display_name, mail_nickname, mail_enabled, security_enabled, description, group_types)`
Creates a new directory group.
### `async list_group_members(group_id, top)`
Lists members of a group.
### `async add_group_member(group_id, user_id)`
Adds a user to a group via POST /groups/{id}/members/$ref with @odata.id payload.
### `async remove_group_member(group_id, user_id)`
Removes a user from a group via DELETE /groups/{gid}/members/{uid}/$ref.
### `async list_applications(top, filter)`
Lists app registrations in the tenant.
### `async list_service_principals(top, filter)`
Lists service principals in the tenant.
### `async list_directory_roles()`
Lists activated directory roles in the tenant.
### `async list_audit_logs(top, filter)`
Lists directory audit log entries.
### `async list_signin_logs(top, filter)`
Lists Entra ID sign-in log entries.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Common HTTP status mapping (via `_STATUS_MAP` or `_classify_failure`):

| Status | ConnectorHealth | AuthStatus |
|---|---|---|
| 401 | OFFLINE | TOKEN_EXPIRED |
| 403 | UNHEALTHY | INVALID_CREDENTIALS |
| 429 | DEGRADED | CONNECTED (logged) |
| 5xx | UNHEALTHY | FAILED (retried) |

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Fix |
|---|---|
| `MISSING_CREDENTIALS` at install | Verify all required install fields populated |
| `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` at health_check | Confirm API user has required permissions in the Microsoft Entra ID dashboard |
| 429 rate limit warnings | The client honours `Retry-After`; reduce request frequency |
